ground.what_this_is: This is the 'Running Chain' workspace, a live map of your active data processing pipeline where nodes are currently executing or waiting.
ground.what_you_can_do: You can click any node to inspect its details, drag connections to link nodes together, and use the top toolbar to change the system's operational mode.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the toolbar region (canvas/app/src/regions/Toolbar.tsx); changing the state logic is a code change there, surfaced via the build-intent wire.
howto.what: The live presence light on the toolbar bridge.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the system's current operational state (running, thinking, or awaiting) at a glance.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the toolbar region (canvas/app/src/regions/Toolbar.tsx); changing its behavior is a code change there, surfaced via the build-intent wire.
howto.what: The 'wire' command button on the bridge's command strip.
howto.what_you_can_do: Click this to initiate a new wire-intent, translating the board's current state into a dispatchable action for the system to execute.

howto.how_to_change: This is a mockup-only display; no built code path exists yet to change it.
howto.what: A question displayed on the canvas asking about the memo gate's function and location.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the question to understand what the system is currently querying or explaining.

howto.how_to_change: This is a mockup-only display of the scheduler logic; no built code path exists yet to change this specific UI element.
howto.what: The memo gate — a status indicator on the Running Chain workspace showing when a node's execution can be skipped due to an unchanged hash.
howto.what_you_can_do: Observe the system's optimization logic: when the node's (type, version, config, inputs) hash is unchanged, the gate indicates the node is not re-running, saving runtime.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the footer region (canvas/app/src/regions/Footer.tsx); changing the text or state indicators is a code change there.
howto.what: The status footer at the bottom of the running workspace.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the live state of the data processing pipeline (e.g., 'live', 'ran ask', 'event-stream connected') and see the resolution count (4/4 resolved).

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the toolbar region (Toolbar.tsx); changing its label or behavior is a code change there.
howto.what: The 'inbox' button on the top toolbar of the Running Chain workspace.
howto.what_you_can_do: Click this to open the inbox triage deck where you can see and manage escalated decisions and approvals.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the canvas region (canvas/app/src/NodeShape.tsx); changing the trigger logic is a code edit there.
howto.what: The 'build' command on the Running Chain workspace.
howto.what_you_can_do: Initiate a new build cycle for the active data processing pipeline, translating the current node state into a fresh execution run.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the toolbar region (canvas/app/src/regions/Toolbar.tsx); changing it is a code change there.
howto.what: The layers control on the toolbar.
howto.what_you_can_do: Open the layers panel to manage the system's structural depth and node organization.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the toolbar region (canvas/app/src/regions/Toolbar.tsx); changing its behavior is a code change there.

howto.what_you_can_do: Click this to immediately force the system into a new operational state, overriding the current mode.

howto.how_to_change: The rack fills itself from the live registry; adding a node-type (nodes/<name>.py) makes a new block appear here automatically, no palette code change required.
howto.what: The 'Node palette' — the rack of build-blocks displaying every known node-type (process, acts, content, holds) grouped by kind.
howto.what_you_can_do: Click a block to drop that node on the board; select two and use the wire tool to connect them.

reasons: accuracy jury (Layer 2, SOFT — variance not error): no quorum that the dossier is grounded in the element (1 of 3 draws agreed grounded). Flagged for operator scrutiny.

howto.how_to_change: The rail is driven by NodeShape.tsx (specifically the NODE-kinds and NODE-types definitions) and the App.tsx shell; adding new chips requires code edits there.
howto.what: The node palette rail on the canvas — the vertical strip of available node types for building the pipeline.
howto.what_you_can_do: Click a node chip (e.g., 'ask', 'llm', 'retrieve') to place that node type onto the canvas board; the rail reflects the current system's supported node kinds.

reasons: accuracy jury (Layer 2, SOFT — variance not error): no quorum that the dossier is grounded in the element (0 of 3 draws agreed grounded). Flagged for operator scrutiny.
